spec_match.rationale: Exercises the core `drawElement()` method on a 2D context by rendering a customized HTML fragment dynamically.

interactive.rationale: Allows real-time editing of the source HTML, rotating, and scaling, updating the canvas on control change or button click.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Correctly aligns with the core purpose of presenting drawElement() as a native replacement for foreignObject or html2canvas.

edge_cases.rationale: Provides SVG fallback for browsers without the API, but doesn't demonstrate edge cases like massive dimensions or invalid HTML structures.

references.rationale: Directly includes the ChromeStatus feature link `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5172548013916160` in the 'see also' list.

design_system.rationale: Integrates cleanly with the shared styles, responsive grid layout, and consistent typography.

openQuestions.title: Demo live element interaction capture
openQuestions.detail: The interactive button in the source HTML says 'Click me' and changes text to 'clicked!', but drawing it on the canvas before/after clicking does not auto-update unless re-clicked. A real-time sync option or automated check for dynamic interaction states would make the demo even more convincing.

summary: An excellent baseline concept demonstrating the fundamental drawElement capability with responsive controls and clean layout, passing all design system and reference rules.
